name: sec-review-marketplace
description: Single-plugin marketplace distributing sec-review: a multi-lane cybersecurity audit plugin covering web services, browser extensions, Rust crates, Go projects, Python projects, Ansible playbooks, shell scripts, networking-as-code (Tor / WireGuard / sing-box / Xray), mobile apps (Android + iOS), desktop apps (Linux + macOS + Windows), Kubernetes manifests, Terraform/Pulumi IaC, GitHub Actions workflows, and virtualization / container runtimes (Docker, Podman / Quadlet, libvirt / QEMU / KVM, Apple Containers, UTM) — with live CVE enrichment and citation-grounded fixes.

plugins.name: sec-review
plugins.description: Twenty-two-agent cybersecurity review pipeline (sec-expert + finding-triager + cve-enricher + report-writer + eighteen tool-lane runners: sast, dast, webext, rust, android, ios, linux, macos, windows, k8s, iac, gh-actions, virt, go, shell, python, ansible, netcfg) with model pinning — sonnet for code reasoning, haiku for high-volume CVE feed I/O and tool dispatch. Multi-stack dispatch is the default: when a project satisfies ≥2 inventory keys (Tauri = Rust + webext + desktop OS; Flutter = Android + iOS; React-Native = Android + iOS + webext), all applicable runners dispatch in parallel and findings render in separate per-lane sections. The `/sec-review` slash command accepts `--only=<lanes>` / `--skip=<lanes>` flags for selective dispatch; the report includes a per-lane summary table and lane-filter metadata. Citation-grounded expertise across databases, frameworks, webservers (incl. Windows/IIS), proxies, frontend, browser extensions (Chrome MV3 + Firefox AMO), Rust/Cargo + unsafe surface, Android (manifest + data + runtime), iOS (Info.plist + codesign), desktop Linux (systemd + sandboxing + packaging), desktop macOS (hardened runtime + TCC + .pkg/Sparkle), desktop Windows (PE hardening + Authenticode + AppLocker/WDAC + MSI/MSIX), Kubernetes admission, Terraform/Pulumi IaC, GitHub Actions, virtualization / alternative-container-runtimes (Docker daemon, Podman / Quadlet, libvirt / QEMU / KVM, Apple Containers, UTM), Go (stdlib security + module ecosystem + web frameworks Gin/Echo/Fiber/Chi/gRPC), shell scripts (command injection, file handling / TOCTOU / Zip Slip, script hardening), Python (deserialization / pickle / yaml.load / XXE; subprocess / async / SSRF; framework deepening for Django ORM, Flask SSTI, FastAPI DI), Ansible (playbook security / Vault handling / role secrets / risky-shell-pipe / no-log-password / partial-become / package-latest), networking-as-code (Tor torrc / hidden services / ControlPort hardening; WireGuard wg-quick conf / AllowedIPs scoping / PSK; sing-box JSON inbound/outbound/Reality; Xray JSON / VMess AEAD / SS-2022 / Reality), auth, TLS, containers, secrets, and supply-chain — all primary-source-cited. Live CVE enrichment from NVD 2.0, OSV.dev (native for crates.io/Maven/NuGet/PyPI/npm/Go/RubyGems/Packagist; best-effort via GHSA for CocoaPods/SwiftPM/Debian), and GHSA with CISA KEV cross-reference. Structured skipped-list distinguishes seventeen canonical clean-skip reasons (no-apk/no-bundle/no-pkg/no-debian-source/no-elf/no-systemd-unit/no-pe/no-containerfile/no-libvirt-xml/no-shell-source/no-requirements/no-playbook/no-singbox-config/no-xray-config/requires-macos-host/requires-systemd-host/requires-windows-host) plus no-notary-profile from tool failures. `references/COVERAGE.md` is the single source of truth enumerating every lane, ecosystem, and skip reason. Per-run token-cost accounting; offline-degradation drills for CVE feeds and all tool lanes; prioritized markdown report with verbatim-quoted primary-source fixes. v1.10 UX improvements: invocation defaults to current working directory when no path is supplied (no more redundant prompting); inventory pass detects known-but-uncovered technologies (Java non-Spring, C/C++, Solidity, PHP, Ruby non-Rails, .NET ASP.NET Core, Lua/LuCI, Elixir, Helm, Jupyter, GitLab/Jenkins/CircleCI, Move/Cairo/Vyper, eBPF, WebAssembly, build systems) and surfaces them as Coverage-gap suggestions in the report.
